Summary: The Crystal Black PlayStation Vita was the launch model of Sony's second handheld console. It featured a 5-inch OLED display, dual analog sticks and powerful hardware designed to deliver console-quality gaming on the go.
The Crystal White PlayStation Vita offered the original OLED model in a clean white finish while retaining identical hardware specifications.
The Cosmic Red PlayStation Vita featured a vibrant metallic red finish and became one of the most recognizable official colour variants.
The Sapphire Blue PlayStation Vita introduced a striking blue colour option for Sony's OLED handheld.
The Ice Silver PlayStation Vita featured a premium metallic finish and was released as an official retail colour.
The PCH-2000 redesigned the PlayStation Vita with a slimmer, lighter body, an LCD display, improved battery life and a micro-USB charging port.
The White PCH-2000 offered the slimmer PlayStation Vita in a clean white finish while retaining the redesigned hardware.
Aqua Blue became one of the most popular colour variants of the slim PlayStation Vita.

Official Name: Sony PlayStation Vita Lime Green / White
Summary: This two-tone PlayStation Vita combined a lime green front with a white rear shell, offering one of the handheld's most distinctive retail finishes.
The Pink / Black PlayStation Vita paired contrasting colours in an eye-catching official retail design.

Official Name: Sony PlayStation Vita Borderlands 2 Limited Edition
Summary: Released alongside Borderlands 2 for PlayStation Vita, this official bundle included a PS Vita system, game and themed packaging for fans of the series.

Edition Name: PCH-2000 Dragon Quest Metal Slime Edition
Official Name: Sony PlayStation Vita Dragon Quest Metal Slime Edition
Summary: This limited edition PlayStation Vita celebrated Dragon Quest with exclusive Metal Slime artwork and themed branding, making it a highly collectible official release.
